description: §65.6 — the negative half. A bare variant cell (`<mode> = .Light`) with NO in-scope `<theme for=@mode>` binding to supply the type context is E-VARIANT-AMBIGUOUS (error). This is the bracketing proof that the `<theme for=>` binding IS the variant-type source (§14.10): remove the theme and the same bare `.Light` cell can no longer be typed. Pairs with `theme-for-variant-inference` (add the theme → the ambiguity clears).
rationale: §65.6: a bare `.Light` with no in-scope `<theme for=>` binding to supply the type context is E-VARIANT-AMBIGUOUS. The theme is the SINGLE owner of the variant set; without it there is no floating enum/type name to resolve `.Light` against. This is the direct negative of the infer-from-`for=` positive — proving the binding, not a convention, carries the type.
